response.justification: James Smith is a capable full-stack and polyglot engineer with genuine distributed systems exposure and a strong testing culture, but he is a poor fit for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the C++ requirement: the job demands 8 years of C++ programming, while James has approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project—neither of which constitutes meaningful production C++ experience. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and C++ does not appear in his listed skills. Beyond that, the role centers on technical leadership of a cloud compute platform, and James has no demonstrated experience leading cloud infrastructure teams, owning compute or storage platform strategy, or architecting at the infrastructure layer. His cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK usage at Optilyz in an application-layer pricing context.
strengths: Genuine distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic (Dunnhumby) building low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong, consistent TDD and software craftsmanship culture including organizing workshops, talks, and training programs
Broad polyglot background across embedded, systems, and web domains demonstrating adaptability and first-principles thinking
concerns: Approximately 6 months of C/C++ work in 2014–2015 versus the mandatory 8-year requirement—the most critical disqualifier for this role
No cloud platform or compute infrastructure leadership experience; background is overwhelmingly web/full-stack application engineering
No demonstrated technical leadership role driving platform strategy, managing engineer growth at scale, or owning large ambiguous infrastructure projects
